title: chaenomeles cardinalis
intro: Chaenomeles cardinalis, also known as the Japanese quince, is a deciduous shrub belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chaenomeles cardinalis include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
